channel.title: CTV News – Queen's Park
channel.description: Queen's Park coverage from CTV News Toronto.

item.title: Doug Ford says Canada must make Donald Trump ‘feel the pain’ following latest tariff threats
Will Stan Cho remain in PC caucus following hotel expense claim scandal? Doug Ford won’t say
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford wouldn’t say Monday if a former minister who stepped down last week after promising to reimburse thousands of dollars in hotel expenses will remain in his caucus, as some critics called for more cabinet members to resign over their expenses.

item.title: ‘I made a mistake:’ Stan Cho resigns from Ford cabinet over Toronto hotel expense claims
item.description: Ontario Tourism, Culture and Gaming Minister Stan Cho has resigned from Premier Doug Ford’s cabinet, saying he made “a mistake” by claiming accommodation expenses for overnight stays in Toronto.

item.title: Ontario asks federal government for potential wildfire evacuation help in the north
item.description: Ontario is requesting assistance from the federal government to get ready to evacuate communities across the north by aircraft as dozens of forest fires burn out of control.

item.title: ‘You’re paying back every single penny’: Ford says ministers must repay taxpayers for Toronto hotel stays
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford says members of his government who billed taxpayers for Toronto hotel stays despite living in and around the GTA will pay back “every single penny” adding that the expenses are “totally unacceptable.”

item.title: Ontario Premier’s Office slashes headcount, initiates hiring freeze to save money
item.description: The chief of staff to the Premier of Ontario has eliminated 10 positions from Doug Ford’s Office and initiated a hiring freeze, a memo obtained by CTV News reveals.

item.title: Ford government looks to axe rule that let Toronto MPPs expense Toronto hotel stays
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford’s government is looking to eliminate a rule that lets members of provincial parliament who live in and near Toronto expense hotel stays in the city, after multiple members of his caucus billed thousands of dollars.

item.title: Stan Cho, tourism minister and Toronto MPP, to repay $16K in city hotel expenses
item.description: An Ontario cabinet minister who represents a north Toronto riding is promising to reimburse more than $16,000 in Toronto hotel expenses.

item.title: Parents slam ‘make-work project’ for private administrator of Ontario’s autism program
item.description: A group representing parents of children with autism says something has to be done to improve dismal waitlist numbers—and they’re targeting what they’re calling a “make-work project” for the private company administering the system.

item.title: Former Ontario integrity commissioner who wrote Greenbelt report dies
item.description: Ontario’s former integrity commissioner, who authored a lengthy report on the provincial government’s Greenbelt scandal, has died a year after retiring.

item.title: Company behind Crown Royal whisky signs deal to sell Ontario bottling plant
item.description: The company behind Crown Royal whisky says it has agreed to sell its bottling plant in southern Ontario.

item.title: Alberta treated ‘like garbage’ by former federal government, Premier Ford says
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford says that Alberta was treated “like garbage” by the former Liberal government in Ottawa.

item.title: Ajax MPP drops out of Ontario Liberal leadership race
item.description: Ontario Liberal MPP Rob Cerjanec has dropped his bid to become the next leader of the party.

item.title: No apparent fines yet for ticket resellers under Ontario price-cap law
item.description: Ontario’s World Cup matches have come and gone with ticket resale platforms running afoul of a new price cap law but seemingly facing no penalties from the province.

item.title: TDSB, TCDSB approve budgets with multi-million-dollar deficits despite staff and program cuts
item.description: Despite some program cuts, Toronto’s two public school boards, which are under provincial supervision, approved their budgets with projected deficits in millions of dollars.
